success_surface: Cell-level text parsing and formatting task with conditional logic on comma-separated values within individual cells
effective_behaviors: Explored workbook structure and confirmed target/source cell contents before implementing changes
Used openpyxl exclusively when formatting requirements exist alongside value modifications
Created modular, reusable parsing functions to handle cell-level string transformations with edge case protection
Applied formatting (fonts, borders) to all cells in target range after data computation completes
Verified computed values across entire result range through post-processing validation output
Implemented case-insensitive pattern matching when filtering by character prefixes
Handled None/null values gracefully in string parsing logic to prevent runtime errors
evidence: Agent ran diagnostic code first to check sheet names, headers, B2:B6 source data, and C2:C6 target emptiness before solution code
Openpyxl used instead of pandas due to formatting requirements (borders, font specifications)
count_valid_bins() function encapsulated parsing logic with empty check, comma splitting, colon extraction, and prefix filtering
Border and font styles defined once then applied via loop over C2:C6 rows after all calculations completed
Printed each resulting cell value after save operation to confirm correct counts (1, 1, 0, 0, 6)
Function returns 0 immediately if input is falsy, preventing attribute errors on None

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Workbook Diagnostics
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure, source data range, and target cell state before modifying content
memory_items.generalization: When working with spreadsheet files, load and examine the relevant sheets, headers, and existing cell values to confirm assumptions about data layout and identify any anomalies. This prevents errors from incorrect range targeting and reveals edge cases early.

memory_items.title: Modular Cell-Level String Processing Functions
memory_items.lesson: Wrap text parsing logic in reusable functions with defensive checks for None values, empty strings, and missing delimiters
memory_items.generalization: For tasks involving string analysis within cell values (parsing delimiters, extracting substrings, conditional counting), create isolated functions that validate inputs, handle empty/malformed data gracefully, and return consistent output types. This improves readability and testability.

memory_items.title: Openpyxl for Combined Value and Formatting Tasks
memory_items.lesson: When a task requires both computing new cell values and applying visual formatting, use openpyxl exclusively rather than mixing pandas for data and openpyxl for style
memory_items.generalization: If the final output must include specific fonts, borders, colors, or number formats alongside updated cell values, load and work entirely through openpyxl's Workbook object. Pandas.to_excel strips all styling information, making post-processing inefficient or impossible.

memory_items.title: Post-Processing Range Verification
memory_items.lesson: After writing values to a target range, iterate through and print/display all modified cells to confirm correct application across the full range
memory_items.generalization: Before considering a spreadsheet modification complete, validate that every cell in the intended output range contains the expected value. Print key cells or perform spot checks to catch off-by-one errors, partial updates, or logic bugs affecting only some rows.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Pre-Execution Workbook Diagnostics
Always inspect workbook structure, source data range, and target cell state before modifying content. Load and examine the relevant sheets, headers, and existing cell values to confirm assumptions about data layout and identify any anomalies. This prevents errors from incorrect range targeting and reveals edge cases early.

### Modular Cell-Level String Processing Functions
Wrap text parsing logic in reusable functions with defensive checks for None values, empty strings, and missing delimiters. For tasks involving string analysis within cell values (parsing delimiters, extracting substrings, conditional counting), create isolated functions that validate inputs, handle empty/malformed data gracefully, and return consistent output types. This improves readability and testability.

### Openpyxl for Combined Value and Formatting Tasks
When a task requires both computing new cell values and applying visual formatting, use openpyxl exclusively rather than mixing pandas for data and openpyxl for style. If the final output must include specific fonts, borders, colors, or number formats alongside updated cell values, load and work entirely through openpyxl's Workbook object. Pandas.to_excel strips all styling information, making post-processing inefficient or impossible.

### Post-Processing Range Verification
After writing values to a target range, iterate through and print/display all modified cells to confirm correct application across the full range. Before considering a spreadsheet modification complete, validate that every cell in the intended output range contains the expected value. Print key cells or perform spot checks to catch off-by-one errors, partial updates, or logic bugs affecting only some rows.
